label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about why Americans don't take all their vacation days even though they feel they need more holidays. The question asks for the reason, and there are four options. First, I need to go through the passage again to identify the key points. The first paragraph mentions that more than half don't take all their days, and even when they take a break, they check emails frequently. The expert Samuel Nahmias is quoted, linking this to the economic situation in the US with high unemployment. He says people are unwilling to take time off because they're afraid of losing their jobs. The passage also compares the US situation to European countries where more vacation time is normal. It's noted that people in the US have less annual leave, and those with jobs have more responsibilities. The majority of workers who don't take time off mention responsibilities or stress, and some don't plan holidays because they don't want to look bad in the current situation. Technology allows them to stay connected, so they don't fully disconnect.
